title: Vacuum photon emission and mean electromagnetic field in pair-creating external backgrounds
authors: I. A. Aleksandrov, E. V. Perelygin, D. V. Chubukov
abstract: We develop a perturbative description of vacuum radiative processes in quantum electrodynamics with a prescribed external electromagnetic background capable of producing electron-positron pairs. Since the initial vacuum is then unstable and the in- and out-vacua are inequivalent, radiative observables require a real-time formulation beyond the ordinary in-out approach of vacuum-stable QED. Using the Keldysh-Schwinger-Fradkin nonequilibrium technique, we derive the mean number density of emitted photons through the second nonvanishing order in the fine-structure constant. The leading term, of order $α$, reproduces the known vertex and tadpole mechanisms, while the complete order-$α^2$ correction contains interference, loop, and induced-current contributions. We also give an independent derivation based on the spectral decomposition of the identity operator in the in-Fock space, where the photon number density is represented as a sum of squared transition amplitudes and vacuum-disconnected terms are canceled by the optical theorem generalized to an unstable vacuum. In addition, we compute the mean electromagnetic field through order $e^3$, including the electromagnetic dressing of the induced vacuum current, and verify it using the corresponding Schwinger-Dyson equations. The final formulas are expressed in terms of exact solutions and propagators of the Dirac equation in the external background and apply to general spacetime-dependent field configurations.
summary: This theoretical paper calculates how photons are emitted from the vacuum and determines the mean electromagnetic field when subjected to a strong, pair-creating external background. It employs advanced real-time (Keldysh) techniques necessary because the initial vacuum state is unstable. The results provide high-order perturbative corrections for these fundamental quantum electrodynamic observables.
main_problem: The paper develops a perturbative description of vacuum radiative processes and calculates the mean electromagnetic field in external, pair-creating electromagnetic backgrounds.
main_result: They derive the mean photon number density up to order alpha^2 and compute the mean electromagnetic field up to order e^3 using non-equilibrium quantum field theory techniques.
method: The primary method is the Keldysh-Schwinger-Fradkin real-time formalism, supplemented by an alternative spectral decomposition approach for consistency checks.
model_or_system: The system is Quantum Electrodynamics (QED) subjected to a general, spacetime-dependent external classical electromagnetic background field capable of inducing electron-positron pair production.
key_observables: Mean photon number density ($\langle N_\gamma angle$) and the mean electromagnetic field ($\langle A_\mu(x) angle$).
important_parameters: Fine-structure constant (alpha), external background field strength, and the critical scale for pair production.
main_assumptions: The initial vacuum is unstable due to the external field, necessitating a real-time formalism beyond standard in-out approaches. Calculations are performed perturbatively in the coupling constant.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate Feynman diagrams contributing to the photon number density calculation at first and second order in alpha, and diagrams describing the mean electromagnetic field correction up to third order in e.
paper_structure: The paper systematically calculates observables by expanding the generating functional on the Keldysh contour. It presents results for $\langle N_\gamma angle$ using diagrammatic methods and an alternative identity-based approach, followed by a detailed calculation of $\langle A_\mu(x) angle$ via Schwinger-Dyson equations.
why_interesting: The use of the Keldysh formalism to calculate expectation values in unstable quantum vacua is crucial for understanding non-equilibrium quantum optics and field theory effects beyond standard vacuum calculations.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1. Two diagrams describing the photon number density (25) to first order in α within the nonequilibrium perturbative framework. The incoming and outgoing photon lines correspond to λ1 = 2 and λ2 = 1, respectively. After the Keldysh-index summation and simplification, they yield Eq. (29).

Figure 2. Ten diagrams describing the photon number density (32) to second order in α within the nonequilibrium perturbative framework. After the Keldysh-index summation and simplification, they yield Eq. (37).
